items.title: Strava blames zero-code AI apps and scrapers as it tightens API access
items.description: The popular fitness-tracking platform, Strava, is restricting access to its API as part of efforts to clamp down on AI scraping, as reported earlier by TechCrunch. Developers who want to build an app using Strava's data now need to pay for a flat $11.99 / month subscription. In an update on its deve...

items.title: Computex 2026: All the news and announcements

items.title: AI is blowing up music. How should the Grammys handle it?
items.description: Today I’m talking with Harvey Mason Jr., who is CEO of the Recording Academy — that’s the outfit that puts on the Grammy Awards. items.title: Microsoft could be the next Big Tech antitrust target

items.title: Xbox and PlayStation have a lot to prove
items.description: Things are bad out there.
